## `struct list_head` fields directly in `struct task_struct` body (`include/linux/sched.h:743`â€“`1554`)

Scanned the full struct body line-by-line (verified with `grep -n "struct list_head"` over lines 743â€“1554); confirmed none are pointers (no `*`) and none sit inside an anonymous nested `struct`/`union` â€” task_struct has no anonymous aggregates at all, only a plain bitfield run (`sched_reset_on_fork:1` etc.).

**Unconditional (7):**
| field | line |
|---|---|
| `tasks` | 870 |
| `children` | 989 |
| `sibling` | 990 |
| `ptraced` | 999 |
| `ptrace_entry` | 1000 |
| `thread_group` | 1005 |
| `thread_node` | 1006 |

**Inside `#ifdef` blocks (7)** â€” each counted once, guard noted:
| field | line | requires |
|---|---|---|
| `rcu_node_entry` | 847 | `CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU` |
| `rcu_tasks_holdout_list` | 856 | `CONFIG_TASKS_RCU` |
| `trc_holdout_list` | 863 | `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| `trc_blkd_node` | 864 | `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| `cg_list` | 1227 | `CONFIG_CGROUPS` |
| `pi_state_list` | 1238 | `CONFIG_FUTEX` |
| `perf_event_list` | 1246 | `CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS` |

**Total: 14 fields** (7 unconditional + 7 gated behind 5 distinct configs).

Note: I tried cross-checking via cbm's graph (`MATCH (c:Class {name:"task_struct"})-[:HAS_FIELD]->(f:Field) WHERE f.type CONTAINS "list_head"`), but its `Field` index returns nothing for `task_struct` â€” only `DEFINES`-type edges from files that merely reference the name. So the list above is from direct source inspection (grep + full read of the struct body), not cbm.

## Example iteration (kernel/)

`kernel/exit.c:471` â€” iterating `task_struct.children` via the `sibling` link field:

```c
// kernel/exit.c:471
list_for_each_entry(c, &p->children, sibling) {
        if (c->mm == mm)
                goto assign_new_owner;
}
```

(Other real uses of the same two fields: `kernel/exit.c:479,702,1499`, `kernel/fork.c:3223`; `ptraced`/`ptrace_entry` pair used at `kernel/exit.c:1513` and `kernel/ptrace.c:618`.)
